encounter.reason: Acute decompensated heart failure after relocation and medication lapse
encounter.service_provider: University of New Mexico Hospital and Heart Failure Clinic
encounter.location: University of New Mexico Hospital, Albuquerque, New Mexico

vitals.name: Oxygen saturation
vitals.timing: on room air on ED presentation

vitals.name: Oxygen saturation
vitals.timing: on 2 L/min nasal cannula after supplemental oxygen

conditions.name: Acute decompensated heart failure with reduced ejection fraction due to medication lapse after relocation
conditions.notes: Known chronic HFrEF with acute exacerbation this encounter; hospitalization for volume-overload decompensation with orthopnea, edema, hypoxemia, BNP elevation, and need for IV diuresis; repeat echocardiogram documented LVEF 33%.

conditions.name: Atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response
conditions.onset_date: known prior to this encounter; acute RVR documented 2024-04-22
conditions.notes: Known paroxysmal atrial fibrillation with acute rapid ventricular response on presentation; initial ECG showed atrial fibrillation with ventricular rate 122 bpm; rhythm later became rate-controlled with intermittent sinus rhythm after treatment and medication restart.

conditions.name: Acute hypoxemic respiratory failure secondary to heart-failure-related volume overload
conditions.notes: Required supplemental oxygen initially, then resolved before discharge.

conditions.name: Chronic kidney disease stage 3b
conditions.notes: Renal function worsened transiently during diuresis with creatinine peak 1.89 mg/dL and eGFR nadir about 30, then improved by discharge to creatinine 1.63 mg/dL and eGFR about 36.

conditions.name: Type 2 diabetes mellitus with hyperglycemia
conditions.notes: Inpatient hyperglycemia documented with glucose 238 mg/dL and hemoglobin A1c 8.8%; inpatient insulin regimen used.

conditions.name: Hypertension
conditions.notes: Severe elevation on presentation was noted, but no new hypertension diagnosis established.

conditions.name: Hyperlipidemia
conditions.notes: Chronic condition acknowledged; statin therapy continued without substantive change.

medications.name: Furosemide 40 mg IV
medications.sig: 40 mg intravenously twice daily during hospitalization
medications.reason: acute decompensated heart failure with volume overload

medications.name: Apixaban 5 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: take 5 mg by mouth twice daily

medications.name: Carvedilol 6.25 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: take 6.25 mg by mouth twice daily
medications.reason: heart failure with reduced ejection fraction and atrial fibrillation rate control

medications.name: Sacubitril/valsartan 24/26 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: take 1 tablet by mouth twice daily
medications.reason: heart failure with reduced ejection fraction

medications.name: Empagliflozin 10 mg oral tablet
medications.reason: type 2 diabetes mellitus and heart failure with reduced ejection fraction

medications.name: Correctional insulin
medications.sig: administer correctional insulin as instructed for inpatient hyperglycemia management

medications.name: Outpatient loop diuretic regimen after discharge
medications.sig: continue outpatient loop diuretic plan as reviewed at discharge

document_references.name: ED note
document_references.description: Emergency department documentation should summarize progressive dyspnea, orthopnea, abdominal bloating, and bilateral leg edema after medication disruption, along with hypertensive tachycardic presentation, hypoxemia, and atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response. It should include the initial exam, oxygen response, laboratory and imaging review, and decision to admit for acute decompensated heart failure.

document_references.name: Admission history and physical
document_references.description: Admission H&P should document known HFrEF, paroxysmal atrial fibrillation, diabetes, CKD stage 3b, hypertension, and hyperlipidemia, with medication lapse after relocation as a likely precipitant. It should capture the volume-overload syndrome, cardiorenal risk, and the initial inpatient treatment plan including IV diuresis, telemetry, oxygen, daily weights, and medication re-establishment.

document_references.name: Daily inpatient progress notes
document_references.description: Progress notes should track response to diuresis, oxygen weaning, renal function changes during decongestion, rhythm control on telemetry, and improvement in dyspnea and edema. They should also reflect re-initiation of guideline-directed medical therapy and ongoing discharge planning.

document_references.name: Discharge summary
document_references.description: Discharge summary should describe hospitalization for acute decompensated HFrEF with atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response and transient hypoxemic respiratory failure, improvement after IV diuresis and medication restart, and renal recovery by discharge. It should include final diagnoses, discharge medications, discharge vitals and weight, and follow-up plans.

document_references.name: Medication reconciliation
document_references.description: Medication reconciliation should document restart of apixaban, carvedilol, and sacubitril/valsartan, continuation of empagliflozin and atorvastatin, inpatient insulin management, and outpatient loop diuretic planning. It should note that prior medication access had been disrupted by relocation.

document_references.name: Heart-failure education materials / discharge instructions
document_references.description: Patient instructions should cover daily weights, sodium restriction to less than 2 g/day, medication adherence, and return precautions for dyspnea, weight gain, edema, chest pain, or palpitations. They should also document planned follow-up with the heart-failure clinic within 1 week.

diagnostic_reports.type: Basic metabolic / renal monitoring during hospitalization

diagnostic_reports.description: CBC was reviewed and showed no leukocytosis, but individual component values were not provided, so no CBC component observations should be created from this note.
Chest X-ray showed pulmonary vascular congestion with small bilateral pleural effusions, supporting heart-failure-related volume overload.
ECG demonstrated atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response at 122 bpm on presentation.
Telemetry later showed rate-controlled atrial fibrillation with intermittent sinus rhythm during hospitalization.
Repeat echocardiogram documented LVEF 33%, global left ventricular hypokinesis, mildly reduced right ventricular function, moderate left atrial enlargement, and no left ventricular thrombus.
